title: noccaea caerulescens subsp. sylvestris
intro: Noccaea caerulescens subsp. sylvestris, also known as alpine penny-cress, is a subspecies of the Noccaea caerulescens plant, belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder. The plant contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Noccaea caerulescens subsp. sylvestris is often used in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential interactions with other medications.
